title: olearia capillaris
intro: Olearia capillaris, commonly known as broom bush or broom heath, is a flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and protect against infections. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Olearia capillaris can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
